content:
- Read the Ralph progress log, refreshed the task summaries, and reviewed the choose-task rules plus the active candidate task files.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, found no explicit <blocked_by> or <priority> tags on the three active failing tasks, and inspected the repo shape enough to ground the pick in the actual stack: top-level Rust workspace plus Go verify service.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-27-verify-operator-ux-reset/04-task-return-full-verify-job-findings-mismatches-and-human-usable-result-json.md as the next task because it is the next operator-facing verify-service gap in the active story chain; task 05 is later cleanup in the same story and story 21 is a separate workflow lane.
- Next turn should execute story-27 task 04 with the tdd skill, then validate make check, make test, and make lint all still pass.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the explicit tdd / improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is story-27 task 04 and moved to inspect the task body plus the boundary-skill materials to decide whether this turn must plan-only or can execute.
- Next should read the task file, inspect code smells around the relevant verify-service boundary, and then follow the task state exactly.

- Read the active story-27 task 04, confirmed it was not yet in a NOW EXECUTE state, and followed the Ralph rule to create a plan instead of starting implementation.
- Reviewed the tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills plus the current verifyservice result/service/error/Rust harness files; identified the main boundary smell as rich typed verify events being collapsed into shallow table buckets and generic mismatch messages.
- Added the plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-27-verify-operator-ux-reset/04-task-return-full-verify-job-findings-mismatches-and-human-usable-result-json_plans/2026-04-20-verify-job-full-findings-result-plan.md, ended it with TO BE VERIFIED, and added the matching <plan> pointer to the task file.
- Next turn should review the proposed public result contract in that plan, flip it to NOW EXECUTE only if the design still looks right, then execute strictly via red-green TDD slices and the boundary cleanup described there.

- Read previous progress plus the active story-27 task pointer.
- Re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill docs for this turn.
- Next I am inspecting the task body and existing plan to determine whether the design is ready to flip from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.

- Read the task file and the existing plan, which currently ends in TO BE VERIFIED.
- The next check is whether the current verify event types and result boundary are rich enough to support the proposed canonical findings contract without log scraping or lossy parsing.

- Verified the Go verify-service side exposes typed inconsistency events with primary keys, mismatch columns, and row values, so the richer contract can be built without scraping logs.
- Next I am checking the Rust job-result contract types to make sure the planned response shape can be extended cleanly before flipping the plan state.

- Read the active story-27 task, the existing plan, the current Go verify-service result boundary, the typed inconsistency event types, and the Rust verify-job contract helpers.
- Confirmed the current event stream already carries primary keys, mismatch columns, row values, and table-level mismatch info, so the planned richer HTTP contract is feasible without log scraping or lossy parsing.
- Flipped the plan file .ralph/tasks/story-27-verify-operator-ux-reset/04-task-return-full-verify-job-findings-mismatches-and-human-usable-result-json_plans/2026-04-20-verify-job-full-findings-result-plan.md from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next turn must read the tdd skill again, execute the plan strictly via red-green slices, and only mark the task done after make check, make lint, and make test all pass.

- Read previous progress, the current task pointer, and re-opened the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill docs for this turn.
- Confirmed the active work is story-27 task 04 and prior progress indicates its plan was already flipped to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task body and the selected plan so I can execute it via red-green slices against the verify job result boundary.

- Read the active task and confirmed the plan is in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn is implementation.
- Inspected the current Go verifyservice result boundary, HTTP tests, service lifecycle code, typed inconsistency events, and the Rust verify job contract helpers.
- Started the first red-green slice by adding a Go HTTP test for a richer success payload; the first test attempt also showed the Go module root lives under cockroachdb_molt/molt rather than the repo root.
- Next I am rerunning that targeted test from the correct Go module root, then I will implement the minimal richer result shape needed for the success slice.

- Finished the first two green slices in Go: a richer success payload and a structured mismatch payload driven from typed verify events.
- Refactored verifyservice/result.go to aggregate canonical findings and render mismatch summaries from those findings instead of exposing legacy transport-only mismatch buckets.
- A broader verifyservice test run now shows the next reds are existing HTTP tests for table-definition mismatch and partial verify-execution failure that still assert the removed legacy fields.
- Next I am updating those existing public-contract tests to the new findings-based shape, then I will continue through unit and Rust contract coverage.

- Finished the Go verifyservice contract work: success, mismatch, table-definition mismatch, and partial verify-execution failure payloads now expose summary/findings/mismatch_summary instead of the removed legacy mismatch lists.
- Added direct result aggregate coverage for row-level mismatch evidence and updated the Rust verify result contract test plus audit support to consume the richer public payload.
- Updated the README verify API examples so operators are pointed at summary, mismatch_summary, and findings in completed job responses.
- Next I am running the required repo-level validation lanes: make check, make lint, and make test. If all pass, I will update the task metadata and finish the Ralph flow.
